---
name: RFC — substantive design decision
about: Architectural change, new variant, new evaluation gate, anything that earns "decision authority"
title: "RFC: <one-line summary>"
labels: rfc
assignees: ''
---

## Problem statement

What is wrong today, with concrete evidence (a run, a metric, a
file path, a test failure). Be specific.

## Design alternatives

Multiple options, with trade-offs. Even when one feels obvious,
articulate the runner-up so the rejection is recorded.

| Option | Sketch | Pro | Con |
|--------|--------|-----|-----|
| A      |        |     |     |
| B      |        |     |     |

## Backtest / validation plan

How will we decide which alternative wins? What experiments,
which data tier (canonical / exploratory), which metrics, which
acceptance gate (Phase-24-equivalent or equivalent).

## Decision criteria

Specific metrics that would change our mind. Pre-register them.

## Cross-references

Related issues, prior diary artifacts, code surfaces.

## Out-of-scope

What this RFC explicitly does not answer.
